title: CLV / CAC Ratio Calculator
premiumFeatures: Benchmarking against industry standards (WERC, Lean Six Sigma)

inputs.label: Average Order Value (AOV)
inputs.businessContext: Mean revenue per transaction. Used in CLV calculation. Typical range: $20-$200 for B2C, $500-$5000 for B2B.

businessContext_i18n.en: Mean revenue per transaction. Used in CLV calculation. Typical range: $20-$200 for B2C, $500-$5000 for B2B.

inputs.label: Purchase Frequency per Year
inputs.businessContext: Number of purchases per customer per year. Derived from historical data or cohort analysis.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of purchases per customer per year. Derived from historical data or cohort analysis.

inputs.label: Customer Lifetime (Years)
inputs.businessContext: Average retention period. For subscription models, use 1/churn rate.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average retention period. For subscription models, use 1/churn rate.

inputs.label: Gross Margin (%)
inputs.businessContext: Profit margin after COGS. Typical range: 30-70% for product businesses, 60-90% for SaaS.

businessContext_i18n.en: Profit margin after COGS. Typical range: 30-70% for product businesses, 60-90% for SaaS.

inputs.label: Total Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
inputs.businessContext: Sum of all marketing and sales costs divided by number of new customers. Includes ad spend, salaries, tools.

businessContext_i18n.en: Sum of all marketing and sales costs divided by number of new customers. Includes ad spend, salaries, tools.

inputs.label: Annual Retention Rate
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of customers retained year over year. Used for churn adjustment. Industry benchmark: 60-90%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of customers retained year over year. Used for churn adjustment. Industry benchmark: 60-90%.

inputs.label: Discount Rate (Annual)
inputs.businessContext: Cost of capital or required rate of return. Typical range: 8-15%.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost of capital or required rate of return. Typical range: 8-15%.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective assessment of input data reliability. Low = estimates, High = audited data.

businessContext_i18n.en: Subjective assessment of input data reliability. Low = estimates, High = audited data.

inputs.label: Include Churn Adjustment
inputs.businessContext: If true, uses retention rate to adjust CLV for churn. Recommended for subscription models.

businessContext_i18n.en: If true, uses retention rate to adjust CLV for churn. Recommended for subscription models.

rules.message: CAC must be greater than zero.
Average order value must be positive.
Gross margin must be between 0 and 100.
Retention rate must be between 0 and 100.
Discount rate must be between 0 and 50.
Customer lifetime must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: CLV/CAC ratio below 3:1 indicates potential inefficiency. Below 1:1 means negative ROI.
Payback period >12 months may strain cash flow. >24 months is critical.
Annual churn >10% is above average. >20% is critical for SaaS.

formulas.name: Annual Revenue per Customer
formulas.description: Total revenue generated by a single customer in one year.

formulas.name: Annual Churn Rate
formulas.description: Proportion of customers lost per year. Used for churn-adjusted lifetime.

formulas.name: Churn-Adjusted Lifetime (Years)
formulas.description: Effective customer lifetime considering churn. If churn is 30%, lifetime = 1/0.3 ≈ 3.33 years.

formulas.name: Gross Customer Lifetime Value (undiscounted)
formulas.description: Total revenue over customer lifetime before margin and discount.

formulas.name: Net Customer Lifetime Value (discounted, margin-adjusted)
formulas.description: Present value of future profit from a customer. Uses annuity formula for discounting.

formulas.name: CLV / CAC Ratio
formulas.description: Primary metric: ratio of customer value to acquisition cost. Target >3:1.

formulas.name: CAC Payback Period (Months)
formulas.description: Months needed to recover CAC from gross profit. Lower is better.

primary.label: CLV / CAC Ratio
primary.description: Primary KPI: Customer Lifetime Value divided by Customer Acquisition Cost. Healthy >3:1.

payback_months.label: CAC Payback Period
payback_months.description: Time to recover CAC from gross profit

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Churn Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Churn above 10% significantly reduces CLV. Consider retention programs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Gross Margin
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Margin below 30% may indicate pricing or cost issues.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Long Payback Period
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Payback >12 months strains cash flow and increases risk.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Indicates high CAC relative to profit.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Discount Rate
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Discount rate above 15% may overstate risk or capital cost.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Reduces present value of future cash flows.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Retention Rate
suggestedActions.description: Implement loyalty programs, customer success, or product improvements to reduce churn by 5%.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce Customer Acquisition Cost
suggestedActions.description: Optimize ad spend, improve conversion funnels, or focus on organic channels.

suggestedActions.label: Increase Average Order Value
suggestedActions.description: Upsell, cross-sell, or bundle products to raise AOV by 10%.

suggestedActions.label: Improve Gross Margin
suggestedActions.description: Negotiate supplier costs, reduce waste (Lean), or increase prices.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: CLV/CAC Ratio (Confidence Adjusted)
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result multiplied by a confidence factor (low=0.8, medium=0.95, high=1.0). Provides conservative estimate.
